filtering_packets: Filtering communication packets through ML nodes
urgent_threats: URGENT: CRITICAL THREATS DETECTED IN INCOMING DATA
marked_safe_msg: Sender whitelisted. Future messages will be treated as safe.
reported_msg: Message flagged as malicious. Thank you for helping improve our detection.
strategic_defense_desc: Security protocols tailored to your assessed risk profile. Follow these measures to strengthen your defense posture.
no_advice_desc: Take the Phish-IQ Quiz to receive personalized security recommendations based on your risk profile.
quiz_load_error: Could not load Phish-IQ Quiz. Please check your internet connection.
quiz_submit_error: Failed to submit quiz results. Please try again.
safe_analysis: No malicious patterns identified in neural screening.
threat_analysis: Detected behavioral anomalies consistent with phishing protocols.
sender_verified: Sender reputation verified against global blacklist signatures.
daily_quiz_desc: New AI-generated questions tested against your language profile.
lesson_1_slide_1_content: Phishing is a cyber attack that uses disguised email as a weapon. The goal is to trick the recipient into believing that the message is something they want or need.
lesson_1_quiz_question: What is the primary goal of a phishing attack?
lesson_2_slide_1_content: Attackers often use URLs that look very similar to legitimate ones, like 'g00gle.com' instead of 'google.com'. Always double-check the spelling.
lesson_2_quiz_question: Which URL is likely a phishing link?
lesson_3_slide_1_content: A strong password should have at least 12 characters and include a mix of uppercase letters, lowercase letters, numbers, and symbols.
lesson_3_quiz_question: Which of these is the strongest password?
lesson_4_s1_c: Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) adds a second layer of security to your online accounts. Verifying your identity using a second factor (like a phone code) makes it much harder for hackers to gain access.
l4_o2: It adds a second layer of defense
lesson_5_s1_c: Social engineering is the art of manipulating people so they give up confidential information. The types of information criminals are seeking can vary, but when individuals are targeted the criminals are usually trying to trick you into giving them your passwords or bank information.
lesson_6_s1_c: Your mobile phone contains more personal data than your computer. Keep it secure with biometrics, regular updates, and only downloading apps from official stores.
lesson_6_q: How can you keep your mobile secure?
lesson_7_s1_c: Public Wi-Fi networks are often unsecured. Hackers can intercept your data as it travels through the air. Avoid accessing sensitive accounts like banking on open networks.
lesson_7_q: What should you avoid on public Wi-Fi?
lesson_8_s1_c: Data privacy is about your right to control how your personal information is collected and used. Be careful about what you share on social media.
lesson_9_s1_c: CEO fraud (BEC) is when an attacker impersonates a high-ranking executive to trick employees into transferring funds or sharing sensitive data.
lesson_10_s1_c: If you think your account is compromised, act fast: Change your passwords, notify your bank, and report the incident to the authorities.
lesson_10_q: What is the first step if you are compromised?
advice_banking: Banking Safety: Remember, banks will NEVER ask for your OTP, PIN, or scan a QR code over the phone or SMS. Always use the official mobile app.
advice_link_safety: Link Safety: Always hover over (or long-press) links to see the real destination. Look out for subtle misspellings like 'g00gle.com' instead of 'google.com'.
advice_personal_info: Personal Info: Be extremely wary of messages asking for your PAN, Aadhar, or full date of birth. Government agencies and big companies rarely ask for these via SMS.
advice_social_engineering: Psychological Tricks: Phishers love using 'Urgency' (Do it now!) and 'Fear' (Account suspended!) to make you panic. Stay calm and double-check through official channels.
advice_perfect_score: Excellent work! You have a strong grasp of phishing tactics. Keep staying vigilant!
